requirements.desc: Ensure nested monorepo paths match across all presets with exact segment boundaries
requirements.note: src/presets/lib/path-match.js introduces hasPathPrefix/hasSegmentPath/hasAnyPathPrefix using (^|/) regex anchoring. Applied across cakephp2 (5 sources), laravel (6), nextjs (2), and symfony (6). monorepo-match-regression.test.js asserts both root-level and nested positive cases for all 19 updated sources.

requirements.desc: Prevent look-alike segment false positives (e.g. ControllerX vs Controller)
requirements.note: Trailing slash in prefix arguments (e.g. 'src/Controller/') ensures the regex does not match 'src/ControllerX/'. NEGATIVE_CASES in monorepo-match-regression.test.js explicitly verify ViewX, ControllerX, appx, and EntityX are rejected.

hasPathPrefix matches via ^ anchor for root-level paths, preserving existing startsWith semantics. Every CASE in the regression test asserts the root-level sample still returns true alongside the nested variant.

All extension checks (.php, .tsx/.jsx, .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx, /(yaml|yml|xml|php)$/) are retained unchanged in every modified match() body alongside the new helper calls. No extension guard was removed or weakened.

laravel/controllers retains !relPath.endsWith('/Controller.php'); cakephp2/config retains specific file guards (AppController.php, AppModel.php, PermissionComponent.php, TitlesGraphController.php) via hasSegmentPath; symfony/config retains .env/.env.local/composer.json; symfony/routes retains prefix-without-trailing-slash for config/routes to match both files and subdirectories.

requirements.desc: Cover all presets with match(relPath) and document unchanged ones
requirements.note: Two independent coverage-audit tests (monorepo-match-regression.test.js and preset-datasources.test.js) enumerate UPDATED_SOURCES (19) and UNCHANGED_SOURCES (16) and verify the union equals all filesystem-detected match(relPath) definitions. specs/165-.../tests/README.md provides human-readable audit documentation.

summary.notes: All 6 requirements are fully satisfied. The shared path-match helper cleanly addresses nested-path matching (R1), boundary safety (R2), and root-level regression preservation (R3) via the (^|/) regex pattern. Extension and naming constraints (R4, R5) are retained verbatim in every modified match() body. Coverage audit (R6) is enforced by two independent filesystem-scan assertions and documented in a README artifact. Unplanned changes fall into two categories: (1) auto-generated documentation regenerations across docs/ and README.md, which are substantial in volume but are expected SDD finalize-pipeline artifacts; (2) SDD workflow tracking files under specs/. Additionally, review.md records three APPROVED suggestions (rename hasPathPrefix, merge duplicate import statements, merge test audit arrays) that were not implemented — these remain open improvement opportunities but do not affect requirement compliance or correctness.
